description: Evaluates the overall quality of generated or modified code.

criteria.name: correctness
criteria.description: Code produces correct results for all specified inputs and edge cases.

scoring.5: Correct for all inputs including edge cases. Handles all specified error conditions.
scoring.4: Correct for all normal inputs. Minor edge case gaps.
scoring.3: Correct for happy path. Some error conditions unhandled.
scoring.2: Partially correct. Fails for some normal inputs.
scoring.1: Fundamentally incorrect. Does not produce expected results.

criteria.name: readability
criteria.description: Code is easy to read, understand, and maintain.

scoring.5: Clear naming, consistent style, appropriate comments, well-structured. A new developer could understand it quickly.
scoring.4: Generally readable with minor style inconsistencies.
scoring.3: Readable but requires some effort. Missing context in complex sections.
scoring.2: Hard to follow. Inconsistent naming or structure.
scoring.1: Very difficult to read. No clear structure or naming convention.

criteria.name: maintainability
criteria.description: Code is modular, follows SOLID principles, and is easy to extend.

scoring.5: Well-decomposed into small functions/classes. Clear separation of concerns. Easy to extend.
scoring.4: Good structure with minor coupling issues.
scoring.3: Reasonable structure but some tight coupling or large functions.
scoring.2: Monolithic or tightly coupled. Changes would require touching many files.
scoring.1: No modularity. Everything in one function or deeply entangled.

criteria.name: error_handling
criteria.description: Errors are handled gracefully without swallowing, leaking, or crashing.

scoring.5: All error paths handled with appropriate actions. No swallowed errors. Clear error propagation.
scoring.4: Most errors handled. Minor gaps in edge case error handling.
scoring.3: Common errors handled. Some paths may swallow or ignore errors.
scoring.2: Inconsistent error handling. Some unhandled exceptions.

criteria.name: testing
criteria.description: Code is accompanied by meaningful tests.

scoring.5: Comprehensive tests covering happy path, errors, and edge cases. Tests are readable and maintainable.
scoring.4: Good test coverage with minor gaps in edge cases.
scoring.3: Basic tests covering happy path and some error cases.
scoring.2: Minimal tests. Only happy path or superficial assertions.

criteria.name: performance
criteria.description: Code does not introduce unnecessary performance issues.

scoring.5: Efficient algorithms and data structures. No N+1 queries, unnecessary allocations, or blocking calls.
scoring.4: Generally efficient with minor optimization opportunities.
scoring.3: Acceptable performance but some inefficiencies (e.g., redundant computations).
scoring.2: Noticeable performance issues that would affect production.
scoring.1: Severe performance problems (infinite loops, O(n^3) where O(n) suffices, etc.).

criteria.name: documentation
criteria.description: Public interfaces are documented; complex logic has explanatory comments.

scoring.5: All public functions/methods documented. Complex logic explained. No obvious missing docs.
scoring.4: Most public interfaces documented. Minor gaps.

output_format.overall_score: float 0-1 (weighted average of criteria scores normalized to 0-1)
output_format.criteria_scores: object mapping criteria name to { score: 1-5, reasoning: string }
